title: Egg Fried Rice
description: A quick and satisfying classic using leftover rice and eggs.
steps: Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Add minced garlic and stir fry for 30 seconds.
Push garlic to the side, crack in the eggs and scramble until just set.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps.
Drizzle soy sauce and sesame oil over the rice and toss everything together.
Garnish with sliced spring onions and serve immediately.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a Middle Eastern staple.
steps: Sauté diced onion in olive oil over medium heat until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic, cumin, paprika and chili flakes. Cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes, season with salt and simmer 10 minutes.
Make 4 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook until whites are just set but yolks are still runny, 5–7 minutes.
Serve with crusty bread or pita.

title: Garlic Butter Rice
description: Simple, aromatic rice that goes with almost anything.
steps: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add rinsed rice and toast for 2 minutes, stirring.
Pour in broth, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
Cover and cook for 18 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
Fluff with a fork and stir in fresh parsley before serving.

title: Tomato Egg Drop Soup
description: A light, comforting Chinese soup ready in minutes.
steps: Bring broth to a boil with minced garlic.
Add diced tomatoes and cook 3 minutes.
Mix cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water, stir into soup to slightly thicken.
Beat eggs and pour in slowly while stirring the soup in circles.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Top with sliced spring onions and serve hot.

title: Pasta Aglio e Olio
description: The Roman classic — pasta with garlic, olive oil and chili. Four ingredients, pure magic.
steps: Cook spaghetti in heavily salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Thinly slice garlic. Warm olive oil in a wide pan over medium-low heat.
Add garlic and chili flakes. Cook slowly until garlic is golden — do not burn.
Add drained pasta and a splash of pasta water. Toss vigorously to emulsify.
Add more pasta water as needed to create a silky sauce.
Toss in parsley, plate and finish with parmesan.

title: Chicken Stir Fry
description: Tender chicken and vegetables in a savory sauce over rice.
steps: Slice chicken into thin strips and toss with cornstarch, salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Cook chicken until golden and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same wok, stir fry onion, garlic and ginger 2 minutes.
Add bell pepper and cook 2 more minutes.
Return chicken, add soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss and serve over rice.

title: French Omelette
description: Silky, custardy and perfectly rolled — a technique worth mastering.
steps: Beat eggs with a pinch of salt until fully combined.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan over medium-low heat.
Pour in eggs. Immediately stir with a fork while shaking the pan.
When eggs are mostly set but still slightly wet, stop stirring.
Roll the omelette toward you using the fork, then tip onto a plate.
Garnish with snipped chives.

title: Lentil Soup
description: Hearty, filling and loaded with protein — a pantry staple soup.
steps: Sauté onion in olive oil until golden, about 8 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin and turmeric. Cook 1 minute.
Add lentils, chopped tomatoes and 4 cups water or broth.
Bring to a boil, then simmer 25 minutes until lentils are very soft.
Blend half the soup for a creamy texture, then stir back in.
Season with salt, squeeze in lemon juice and serve.

title: Guacamole
description: Fresh, chunky and ready in 5 minutes.
steps: Halve avocados, remove pits and scoop flesh into a bowl.
Mash with a fork to your preferred texture.
Finely dice tomato, onion, jalapeño and cilantro. Add to bowl.
Squeeze in lime juice and season generously with salt.
Mix gently and taste for seasoning.
Serve immediately with tortilla chips or as a topping.

title: Pasta Carbonara
description: A rich Roman classic — creamy without a drop of cream.
steps: Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Fry diced bacon until crispy. Add crushed garlic for the last minute.
Whisk eggs with grated parmesan and a lot of black pepper.
Remove pan from heat. Add drained pasta to the bacon.
Off heat, pour egg mixture over pasta, tossing quickly and adding pasta water to create a silky sauce.
Serve immediately with extra parmesan and pepper.

title: Greek Salad
description: Crisp vegetables, olives and feta — no cooking needed.
steps: Chop tomatoes into wedges and cucumber into chunky pieces.
Thinly slice red onion and add to a large bowl.
Add olives and crumble or place feta on top in a slab.
Drizzle generously with olive oil.
Sprinkle oregano and season with salt.
Toss lightly before serving — or serve untossed, Greek style.

title: Banana Pancakes
description: Two-ingredient pancakes that are naturally sweet and gluten-free.
steps: Mash bananas very well in a bowl until smooth.
Add eggs, cinnamon and vanilla. Mix until combined.
Heat butter in a non-stick pan over medium-low heat.
Pour small rounds of batter into the pan.
Cook 2 minutes until bubbles form, then flip carefully.
Cook 1 more minute and serve with honey or fruit.

title: Dal Tadka
description: Yellow lentils tempered with spiced butter — a staple of Indian cooking.
steps: Rinse lentils and pressure cook or boil with turmeric until very soft.
Sauté onion in oil until golden brown, about 10 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger and tomatoes. Cook until tomatoes break down.
Mix in the cooked lentils with water to reach desired consistency.
Simmer 5 minutes and season with salt.
Make the tadka: melt butter, add cumin seeds and dried chilies until sizzling. Pour over dal.

title: Avocado Toast
description: The brunch icon — ready in 5 minutes.
steps: Toast bread until golden and sturdy.
Halve avocado, remove pit and scoop into a bowl.
Mash with lemon juice and a generous pinch of salt.
Spread avocado mixture thickly over toast.
Top with chili flakes and any optional toppings.
If adding eggs, poach them and place on top. Season and serve.

title: Tomato Basil Soup
description: Velvety, vibrant and warming — better than anything from a can.
steps: Roast tomatoes with garlic and olive oil at 200°C for 25 minutes until caramelized.
Sauté onion in a pot until soft.
Add roasted tomatoes (with all juices) and 2 cups water or broth.
Simmer 10 minutes, then add fresh basil.
Blend until smooth. Add cream if using.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve with crusty bread.

title: Chicken Quesadillas
description: Crispy tortillas loaded with melty cheese and seasoned chicken.
steps: Season chicken with cumin, paprika, salt and pepper. Cook in a pan until done. Shred.
Sauté onion and bell pepper until softened.
Place a tortilla in the pan over medium heat. Add cheese, chicken and vegetables on one half.
Fold the other half over and press gently.
Cook 2 minutes per side until golden and cheese melts.
Slice into wedges and serve with sour cream and salsa.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Creamy, rich Italian rice dish — a labour of love worth every stir.
steps: Sauté mushrooms in butter until golden. Season and set aside.
In the same pan, soften onion and garlic in butter.
Add rice and toast 2 minutes until translucent at the edges.
Pour in wine and stir until absorbed.
Add hot broth one ladle at a time, stirring constantly until each is absorbed.
After 18 minutes, stir in mushrooms, parmesan and remaining butter. Rest 2 minutes before serving.

title: Spinach & Feta Scramble
description: Protein-packed eggs with wilted spinach and creamy feta.
steps: Melt butter in a pan over medium heat. Add minced garlic.
Add spinach and wilt for 1 minute.
Beat eggs with salt and pour into the pan.
Stir gently with a spatula, cooking over medium-low heat.
When eggs are almost set, crumble in feta.
Remove from heat (residual heat finishes cooking) and serve on toast.

title: Beef Tacos
description: Juicy, spiced ground beef in warm tortillas with all the toppings.
steps: Brown ground beef in a pan over high heat, breaking it up.
Add onion and garlic, cook until softened.
Stir in cumin, paprika, salt and pepper.
Add diced tomatoes and cook until sauce thickens, 5 minutes.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan or directly over a gas flame.
Fill with beef, squeeze lime juice and add your favourite toppings.

title: Yogurt Flatbreads
description: Soft, pillowy flatbreads made with just two ingredients — no yeast needed.
steps: Mix flour, yogurt and salt until a dough forms.
Knead lightly for 1 minute until smooth. Rest 5 minutes.
Divide into 6 balls and roll each into a thin oval.
Cook in a dry non-stick pan over high heat, 1–2 minutes per side until charred spots appear.
Brush with melted butter while hot.
Serve with hummus, curries or use as a wrap base.
